definition: Contact between sense field, object, and consciousness, giving rise to felt experience.
notes: The project prefers contact because it is both readable and precise enough for dependent-origination and sense-field analysis. Sensation is discouraged because it collapses phassa into what follows from it rather than the contact event itself.

context_rules.context: In most doctrinal and dependent-origination contexts

context_rules.notes: Use sparingly when the tactile metaphor matters.

example_phrases.pali: cakkhuñca paṭicca rūpe ca uppajjati cakkhuviññāṇaṃ ... phasso
example_phrases.translation: dependent on eye and forms, eye-consciousness arises ... contact
example_phrases.notes: Shows phassa as event arising from conjunction.
